_meta.description: R2B artist-name diagnostic: Wu-Tang Clan (East Coast hip-hop). Artist named in caption. 1 seed. Locked 2026-05-17.

captions.ace_step: East Coast hip-hop song in the style of Wu-Tang Clan, 92 BPM, dusty soul sample loop, sparse piano stab, boom-bap drums, multiple male rapper voices with distinct flows, kung fu sample interludes, gritty street mood, lo-fi sample-based production

lyrics:
[Intro]

[Verse]
Concrete teaching mathematics that the schoolbooks never had
Brother on the corner cooking up a different kind of math
Sirens in the distance like an alarm clock for the kingdom
Every rooftop holding council, every alley holding court

[Chorus]
We the ones the city built around
We the ones the city tried to drown
We the ones still standing in the sound
And we ain't going nowhere right now

[Verse]
Pops told me real recognize real before I knew his real name
Mama held the family on a wage that wouldn't fit the frame
Every cousin got a story that the news ain't never telling
Every block remembers names that nobody else is spelling

[Chorus]
We the ones the city built around
We the ones the city tried to drown
We the ones still standing in the sound
And we ain't going nowhere right now
